name: codecarbon
description: Estimate and track carbon emissions from your computer, quantify and analyze their impact.

codecarbon.launchOnStartup.description: Launch the codecarbon tracker on startup.

codecarbon.interpreter.description: Python executable path. Leave empty to use system default.

codecarbon.autoInstall.description: Automatically install/repair the Python codecarbon package when missing.

codecarbon.installStrategy.description: Package installation strategy for Python codecarbon package.
enumDescriptions: Use current interpreter or active virtual environment first, with --user fallback.
Use custom pip arguments from codecarbon.customPipArgs.

codecarbon.customPipArgs.description: Custom pip install arguments used when codecarbon.installStrategy is custom (example: --index-url <url> --trusted-host <host>).

codecarbon.notifications.description: Notification policy for extension popups. 'default' shows start/stop info and setup warnings; 'minimal' only shows blocking errors.
enumDescriptions: Only show blocking errors as popups. Use output logs for diagnostics.
Show start/stop info popups and warnings for recoverable setup issues.

codecarbon.emissionsFile.description: Optional path for emissions CSV output. Use an absolute path for a central file shared across workspaces, or a relative path resolved from the workspace.

scripts.package:ci: npm run lint && npm run test && npm run compile && vsce package
scripts.publish:vsce: npm run lint && npm run compile && vsce publish
scripts.test: npm run test:smoke && npm run test:python && npm run test:ts
